title: WebP CVE writeup — Huffman lookup-table heap overflow (part 1)
html:
Reading capture from the libwebp Huffman table heap overflow CVE writeup (CVE-2023-4863 territory) — explains how the decoder pre-allocates fixed kTableSize lookup tables for red/green/blue/alpha/distance Huffman channels, references Mark Adler's enough.c zlib tool, and shows the exploit primitive: an attacker-supplied incomplete-binary-tree Huffman table forces over-allocation of second-level tables, blowing past the pre-computed bound and overflowing the heap. Pulled the same week as the buffer overflow quiz — Yahir is reading the canonical real-world example to ground the textbook material.

TL;DR

  • libwebp Huffman heap overflow — decoder pre-allocates FIXED_TABLE_SIZE + N per-channel; attacker-built Huffman tree exceeds the bound and overflows.
  • Concrete numbers: red/blue/alpha tables are 630, distance is 410, green depends on color-cache size (256 + 24 + 0..2048).
  • Cites Mark Adler's enough.c as the tool that computes the worst-case lookup-table size.
  • Paired with the same-day part 2 capture showing the controllable value field + GetNextKey bit-reversal write-position calculation.
  • Real-world grounding for OSU CS security curriculum's buffer overflow unit.

webp cve · buffer overflow · cs coursework · reverse engineering · osu
